description_long: This object is a solar energy infrastructure installation featuring a large, rectangular solar panel array elevated above a patterned platform. The solar panels are supported by a robust metal frame with angled supports, providing stability and optimal tilt for sunlight capture. The base platform is wide and flat, with geometric surface patterns and raised edges, possibly for structural reinforcement or to delineate the installation area. The structure appears to be designed for ground mounting, with the solar array elevated to allow airflow and maintenance access beneath. No visible electrical inverters or cabling are present, suggesting these components may be housed internally or out of view. The overall design is utilitarian, prioritizing function and durability for urban or industrial environments.
description: A large, elevated solar panel array mounted on a patterned, reinforced platform with angled metal supports. The structure is robust and utilitarian, suitable for urban solar energy generation.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the solar panel array and the supporting angled metal frame above the patterned platform.
description_view_1: End view highlighting the width of the platform and the rectangular solar panel array, with the support structure visible beneath.
description_view_2: Opposite side view mirroring view 0, showing the solar panel array and the geometric platform details.
description_view_3: Frontal view focusing on the patterned platform surface and the face of the solar panel array above.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 30 degrees, azimuth direction not clearly specified, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted with angled steel or aluminum frame supports
attribute_array_configuration: single linear row of approximately 6 large rectangular panels
